intro: Leachia meloformis, also known as Melodinus meloformis, is a plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family, commonly referred to as the dogbane or oleander family. Leachia meloformis is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Leachia meloformis include melodinin, meloformisine, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Leachia meloformis is often used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever and digestive issues.
